text.title: Objection FAQ Library Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Objection FAQ Library MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Objections MUST come from real lost-deal artifacts (>=10). Invented or assumed objections are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Real-customer language is what closes; agency-generated FAQ blocks read as marketing fluff.

rule.statement: Each rebuttal MUST cite evidence (case study, customer quote, benchmark, screenshot). Un-backed rebuttals are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without evidence, rebuttals are claims vs. claims; evidence shifts the burden of proof.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST tag funnel-stage (ToFu / MoFu / BoFu). Stage-blind libraries waste copy at wrong stages.
rule.rationale: ToFu objections (trust, fit) differ from BoFu (timing, budget); tagging routes copy correctly.

rule.statement: Library MUST include >=2 copy variants per objection (proposal-inline + landing-page FAQ). Single-variant libraries cannot serve both surfaces.
rule.rationale: Proposal copy and FAQ-block copy differ in voice + length; one-variant libraries force rewrite at use.

rule.statement: Library MUST be refreshed monthly with new lost-deal evidence. Stale libraries drift from current objections.
rule.rationale: Buyer objections shift with market conditions (recession / AI hype / category-creation); monthly refresh keeps copy live.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
